items.title: Approximation, Learning, and Computation in Neural Networks — Barron (1994)
items.author: Andrew R. Barron
items.note: Discusses approximation rates and complexity of neural nets — complements pure UAT existence with data-efficiency perspective.

items.title: Multilayer feedforward networks are universal approximators — Hornik, Stinchcombe, White (1989)
items.author: Kurt Hornik, Maxwell Stinchcombe, Halbert White
items.note: Seminal proof that standard multilayer feedforward networks with squashing activations can approximate Borel-measurable functions — foundational UAT reference.

items.title: Approximation by superpositions of a sigmoidal function — Cybenko (1989)
items.author: George Cybenko
items.note: Classic result showing sigmoid networks are dense in continuous functions on compact sets — often cited alongside Hornik et al.

items.title: Deep Learning — Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville (MIT Press)
items.author: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville
items.note: Chapter 6 discusses universal approximation properties of feedforward networks and connects expressivity to practical deep learning.
